column1: Say, you decide. ""I think I'll get this thing outside... and FAST!
column2: 「說,」你打定主意。「我看我最好趕快把這東西弄到外面……愈快愈好!」

column1: Hey! Stay outta there! shouts the waitress. "You tryin' to get yourself killed?"
column2: 「喂!離那邊遠一點!」女服務生喊道。「你是想找死嗎?」

column1: Say! There's a bobby pin buried in the gravy.
column2: 咦!肉汁裡埋了一根髮夾。

column1: It looks like it might be edible.
column2: 看起來似乎還能吃。

column1: The moving sidewalks are a refreshingly modern touch in such a small airport, but boy, is it a long way to the gates!
column2: 這麼小的機場居然有電動走道,還挺摩登的,不過天啊,到登機門的路還真遠!

column1: The one to your right is labeled: "Brokin" (sic). The machine to the left is labeled: "Flight Insurance, $100."
column2: 你右邊那台上寫著:「顧障」(原文如此)。左邊那台則寫著:「飛航保險,一百元。」

column1: (It's a little large for an insurance machine, isn't it?)
column2: (這台保險機好像有點太大台了,不是嗎?)

column1: It's just a cheap plastic laminate, with a delicate little design that's too small to show up at this resolution!
column2: 只是廉價的塑膠貼皮,上面有精緻小巧的圖案,不過解析度太低根本看不出來!

column1: Nah. You'd rather not.
column2: 算了,你不想那麼做。

column1: (Trust me on this one!)
column2: (這件事相信我就對了!)

column1: MENU   Today only:  Blue P ate Special    $1.00
column2: 菜單   本日限定:  藍 頭頂 特餐    1.00 元

column1: At least the price is reasonable.
column2: 至少價錢還算合理。

column1: A waitress cruises behind the snack bar while a pair of moving sidewalks disappear into the distance. There are some vending machines along the east wall and near the snack bar is a sign.
column2: 一名女服務生在點心吧檯後方穿梭,兩條電動走道則向遠處延伸消失。東側牆邊擺著幾台販賣機,點心吧檯附近還立著一塊招牌。

column1: Pardon me, ma'am, you say, "but do you have a menu?"
column2: 「不好意思,小姐,」你說,「請問有菜單嗎?」

column1: Ain'cha got no eyes, fella? What's that big thing hanging on the wall there?
column2: 「你沒長眼睛啊,帥哥?牆上掛的那塊大招牌是什麼?」

column1: Ha, ha, I fooled you, you tell the waitress. "I'm not eating that stuff again."
column2: 「哈哈,騙你的啦,」你對女服務生說。「我才不要再吃那玩意兒了。」

column1: Why not? she asks, "we say it's just like momma used to make!"
column2: 「為什麼不要?」她問,「我們都說這味道跟老媽做的一模一樣!」

column1: Say, is this stuff good to eat? you ask.
column2: 「這東西好吃嗎?」你問道。

column1: (She does not respond.)
column2: (她沒有回應。)

column1: Hey, mama, hurry up with that chow, you order.
column2: 「喂,美女,那份吃的快點弄好,」你催促道。

column1: (It affects her speed not in the least!)
column2: (完全沒讓她加快半點速度!)

column1: Lovely weather, eh? you ask the waitress.
column2: 「今天天氣真好,對吧?」你對女服務生說。

column1: Yep.
You already did that.
column2: 你已經做過那件事了。

column1: (And you don't want another!)
column2: (你也不會想再來一次!)

column1: You must have some food before you can take it.
column2: 你得先有食物才能拿。

column1: Nah. The plate is stuck to the counter!
column2: 算了。盤子黏在櫃檯上!

column1: No way. You feel that you've tasted that stuff in an earlier lifetime, and that time was enough!
column2: 才不要。你覺得上輩子已經嚐過那玩意兒了,那一次就夠了!

column1: You must have some food before you can eat it. Try ordering something.
column2: 你得先有食物才能吃。試著點份餐吧。

column1: Eat what?
column2: 吃什麼?

column1: This time, you decide to save yourself from a fate worse than (well, ok, actually quite similar to; no, indeed, I suppose it would be identical to) death by fishing the bobby pin out of the "Blue Pate Special's" gravy-like substance instead of swallowing it.
column2: 這次,你決定把「藍頭頂特餐」裡那坨肉汁狀物質中的髮夾夾出來,而不是直接吞下去,好讓自己逃過比死亡還慘(呃,好吧,其實差不多慘;不,說真的,我看根本一模一樣慘)的下場。

column1: This machine is clearly labeled, "Brokin!"
column2: 這台機器上清清楚楚寫著:「顧障!」

column1: It's empty now.
column2: 現在是空的。

column1: (But it did swallow another of your hundred dollar bills!)
column2: (不過它確實吞掉了你另一張百元大鈔!)

column1: You peel off another hundred-dollar bill from your wad of money and insert it into the slot; the machine slurps it up, digests it, considers its authenticity, finds it valid, then begins to whir and shake.
column2: 你從錢卷裡撕下另一張百元大鈔,塞進投幣口;機器把鈔票吸了進去,消化一番,鑑定真偽,確認無誤後,便開始嗡嗡作響、震動起來。

column1: What kind of airline is this, any way? Well, you suppose a parachute would be pretty good insurance, so you pick it up off the floor and hide it in your inner suitcoat pocket!
column2: 這到底是哪門子的航空公司啊?算了,你想降落傘應該算是不錯的保險,於是把它從地上撿起來,藏進西裝內袋裡!

column1: Hey, baby! you shout. "Gimme one o' them 'Blue Plate Specials!'"
column2: 「嘿,美女!」你喊道。「給我來一份『藍盤特餐』!」

column1: That's 'Blue PATE' to you, baldie! she retorts.
column2: 「跟你說是『藍頭頂』特餐啦,禿子!」她反駁道。

column1: Yo, she yells back into the kitchen, "slop me up another bald one!"
column2: 「喂,」她朝廚房喊,「再來一份禿頭特餐!」

column1: Here ya go, big boy, she says with a toothy grin, "one 'Big Blue' comin' atcha."
column2: 「來吧,大帥哥,」她咧嘴一笑,露出滿口牙,「一份『藍色巨無霸』上桌囉。」

column1: She slings the plate on the counter before you.
column2: 她把盤子往你面前的櫃檯一放。

column1: You quickly wolf down the "Blue Pate Special" as if you had not eaten for an entire adventure game. "Say," you tell the waitress, "this is pretty good stuff. Why, this tastes just like the meatloaf Mama Laffer usggheddf dfgggfssef pssqfufufaaf arrrrgggggggghhhhh!"
column2: 你狼吞虎嚥地吃起「藍頭頂特餐」,彷彿整場冒險遊戲裡都沒吃過東西似的。「這個嘛,」你對女服務生說,「味道還真不錯。哎呀,這味道跟拉夫媽媽做的肉捲一樣咦嗯咕嗚嘎嘎嗚啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊!」

column1: You drop to the floor near the counter while making clever, creative sounds with your trachea.
column2: 你倒在櫃檯邊的地上,喉嚨裡發出各種富有創意的怪聲。

column1: You now remember what it was you hated about Mama Laffer's cooking...
column2: 你現在想起來了,你最討厭拉夫媽媽做菜的哪一點……

column1: She had a habit of losing her bobby pins in the gravy, you think, while you frantically make the universal sign for choking.
column2: 「她有個習慣,總是把髮夾弄丟在肉汁裡,」你心想,同時拚命比出全世界通用的哽到手勢。

column1: (What a shame the waitress never heard of Dr. Heimlich!)
column2: (真可惜,這位女服務生從沒聽過哈姆立克急救法!)

column1: This is certainly a long terminal.
column2: 這航廈還真是長。

column1: Your mind wanders...
column2: 你開始神遊……

column1: Previously, you believed that was just a figure of speech!
column2: 先前你還以為那只是一句成語而已!

column1: Finally, you reach the end of the moving sidewalk, and enter the waiting room for Gate #1.
column2: 你終於走到電動走道的盡頭,進入第一號登機門的候機室。

column1: Hey! Stay off that! shouts the gate attendant.
column2: 「喂!別踩上去!」登機門地勤喊道。

column1: Here's my ticket, you say, handing your ticket folder to the gate attendant, "is this Gate #1?"
column2: 「這是我的機票,」你把票夾遞給地勤,「這裡是第一號登機門嗎?」

column1: It certainly is, he replies, "but this ticket is for a flight that took off 30 seconds ago. Too bad you missed it!"
column2: 「沒錯,」他回答,「不過這張票的班機三十秒前就起飛了。可惜你錯過囉!」

column1: Oh, well, says the gate attendant. "%s"
column2: 「唉,算了,」地勤說。「%s」

column1: Good day, sir, you tell the gate attendant. %s
column2: 「日安,」你對地勤說。%s

column1: May I please purchase a ticket?
column2: 「請問我可以買票嗎?」

column1: He responds, "Tickets are only available at the front desk."
column2: 他回答:「機票只能在櫃檯購買。」

column1: How nice for you, he responds. "Are you going to give me your ticket or did you ride that walkway just so we could have this stimulating conversation?"
column2: 「真替你高興,」他回答。「你到底要不要把票給我,還是你搭那條走道過來,純粹是為了跟我聊這段精彩對話?」

column1: Nah. One of these is enough!
column2: 算了。一份就夠了!

column1: That's a good idea. Take a little free reading material to help you wile away your flight time! You grab a brochure from the display and shove it in your pocket, promising to read it when you get to your seat. It seems the perfect choice for a long, boring flight.
column2: 好主意。拿份免費讀物打發飛行時間吧!你從展示架上抓了一本小冊子塞進口袋,心想等坐定位後再來看。這對又長又無聊的飛行來說,似乎是最完美的選擇。

column1: The man by the window is totally absorbed by the fascinating baggage handlers.
column2: 靠窗的男人正全神貫注地盯著那些「精彩絕倫」的行李搬運工。

column1: But... behind the counter waits the man you came to see: the gate attendant.
column2: 但是……櫃檯後面,正是你要找的人:登機門地勤。

column1: There is a computer terminal that is out of order and a display of religious pamphlets on the left end of the counter.
column2: 櫃檯左端有一台故障的電腦終端機,還有一個擺著宗教小冊子的展示架。

column1: There is nothing else of interest on the counter.
column2: 櫃檯上沒有其他值得注意的東西了。

column1: There is a display on the left end of the counter filled with religious tracts.
column2: 櫃檯左端有個展示架,擺滿了宗教小冊子。

column1: That woman looks like she almost screwed herself out of a seat!
column2: 那女人看起來差點就把自己的座位給搞丟了!

column1: The woman seems quite busy with her children.
column2: 那女人似乎正忙著照顧她的孩子們。

column1: All flights are on time.
column2: 所有班機都準時起飛。

column1: (Remember: this IS a fantasy!)
column2: (別忘了:這可是幻想空間!)

column1: There is an airline attendant waiting behind the counter, and a locked door that you presume leads to the jetway -- and your escape!
column2: 櫃檯後面有位空服地勤候著,還有一扇上鎖的門,你猜那應該通往空橋——也就是你的逃生之路!

column1: It certainly is, says the gate attendant, "but if you want to take the flight listed on this ticket, you'd better hurry. I've already announced the final boarding call."
column2: 「沒錯,」地勤說,「不過你要是想搭這張票上的班機,最好動作快點。我已經廣播最後登機通知了。」

column1: The stewardess onboard will give you your seat assignment, says the gate attendant. "%s"
column2: 「機上的空服員會告訴你座位號碼,」地勤說。「%s」

column1: You have no reason to return there. Hurry, Larry; you don't want to miss your plane!
column2: 你沒有理由再回到那裡了。快點,賴瑞,你可不想錯過班機!

column1: This jetway stretches between the airport waiting room and your flight to freedom.
column2: 這條空橋連接著機場候機室,通往你邁向自由的班機。

column1: (But, where are you going?)
column2: (不過,你到底要去哪裡?)

column1: There's nothing behind the curtain that you need.
column2: 布簾後面沒有你需要的東西。

column1: There are two doors here, one leading to the outside and the other to the cockpit.
column2: 這裡有兩扇門,一扇通往外面,另一扇通往駕駛艙。

column1: First Class is certainly more comfortable than "Cattle Class!"
column2: 頭等艙果然比「沙丁魚艙」舒服多了!

column1: The door to the cockpit is at the front of the airplane.
column2: 駕駛艙的門在飛機的最前端。

column1: Is there any one here who would like to trade seats? you ask.
column2: 「這裡有沒有人想跟我換座位?」你問道。

column1: NO! they yell back in unison.
column2: 「不要!」大家異口同聲喊道。

column1: This door was locked from the outside before takeoff and there's no way you're ever going to open it!
column2: 這扇門在起飛前就已經從外面鎖上了,你根本不可能打得開!

column1: Welcome aboard, sir! says the stewardess with her best plastic smile. "May I see your ticket, please?"
column2: 「歡迎登機,先生!」空服員擠出最完美的職業笑容說道。「請問可以看一下您的機票嗎?」

column1: You give it to her.
column2: 你把票遞給她。

column1: Your seat is on your left, in the second compartment Mr. uh, Laffer she says, keeping your ticket.
column2: 「您的座位在左手邊,第二個艙區,呃,拉夫先生,」她邊說邊收下你的機票。

column1: Say! Haven't I seen you on TV? Weren't you the big winner of the Lucky Life Lottery? she asks.
column2: 「咦!我是不是在電視上看過你?你該不會就是那個中了『幸運人生樂透』頭獎的人吧?」她問道。

column1: Yep, that's me, baby, you grin, and hand her a $100 bill. "And here's a little something for you."
column2: 「沒錯,就是我,寶貝,」你咧嘴一笑,遞給她一張百元大鈔。「這個給你,一點小意思。」

column1: It appears that what I've heard is true, she says.
column2: 「看來我聽到的傳聞是真的,」她說。

column1: About me, gorgeous? you respond.
column2: 「傳聞我的事嗎,美人?」你回應道。

column1: No, about the nouveau riche. They ARE incredibly crude!
column2: 「不,是關於暴發戶的傳聞。他們真的是粗俗到不行!」

column1: She gives you her best phony smile. "%s"
column2: 她給了你一個最假的笑容。「%s」

column1: Why, this plane isn't so small! you think. "Look at all the room in here."
column2: 「哎呀,這架飛機其實沒那麼小嘛!」你心想。「你看這裡空間多大。」

column1: Carrumba! somebody screams, "It's one of those wacko foreign terrorists!"
column2: 「我的媽呀!」有人尖叫,「是那種瘋狂的外國恐怖份子!」

column1: Grab the fire ax!
column2: 「快拿消防斧!」

column1: Got him, el Capitan!
column2: 「抓到他了,機長!」

column1: What a waste, Larry! To come so far and then try to hijack an airliner!
column2: 真是白費工夫,賴瑞!都走到這一步了,居然還想劫機!

column1: Whenever you need a stewardess, they're never around.
column2: 每次你需要空服員的時候,她們總是不見人影。

column1: Why is it they only serve drinks whenever you want to walk around the plane?
column2: 為什麼每次你想在機艙裡走動時,他們偏偏就在發飲料?

column1: There's no way you can affect them!
column2: 你根本拿他們沒轍!

column1: There's nothing there but an airsick bag.
column2: 那裡除了嘔吐袋之外什麼都沒有。
